rationale: Empirically verified. Dolt has epic mindspec-3d3i + all 6 beads closed; committed HEAD:.beads/issues.jsonl has epic=open, .6=in_progress — the close_reason fields themselves cite the post-merge re-import. .git/hooks/post-merge runs `bd hooks run post-merge` which imports JSONL->Dolt on every merge/FF; no post-checkout hook exists, so only merge/pull re-imports. Chore-PR (a) is durably correct and idempotent: bd auto-exports Dolt->JSONL after writes, so the working tree ALREADY carries all-7-closed; committing it makes future re-imports a no-op. Diagnosis (b) is accurate — ApproveImpl closes the epic in Dolt (mutation 1/3) but FinalizeEpic's `pr` strategy means main merged via PR #158 BEFORE the close, orphaning the finalize JSONL on protected main. Filing it as a bead is acceptable for THIS minor release (pre-existing, not a 106 regression, deterministic recovery), but it is a recurring user-facing defect that warrants a known-issue release note + high priority. State otherwise consistent: only the two named ids diverge; sc0w/3jq7 genuinely open.

findings.issue: Self-revert diagnosis empirically confirmed: Dolt closed for epic mindspec-3d3i + all 6 beads; committed HEAD JSONL has epic=open and .6=in_progress; post-merge shim execs `bd hooks run post-merge` (JSONL->Dolt import). The ONLY incorrect divergence in the whole tracker is these two ids; all other committed-open ids are genuinely open in Dolt too. No other spec/epic is stuck.

Chore-PR (a) durably fixes the revert. No post-checkout hook is installed, so checking out the chore branch will NOT re-import; only merge/pull does, and by then main's JSONL is correct -> the re-import is idempotent (no-op). The corrected JSONL is already auto-exported in the working tree (status M, all 7 closed).

When authoring the chore PR, generate the JSONL FROM the closed Dolt state (bd export) and verify Dolt still shows all closed immediately before committing. Guard against an intervening `git pull`/merge re-importing the stale main JSONL into Dolt mid-authoring, which would then export the reverted (open) state. The current working-tree blob is already correct — committing it directly is the safest path.

The impl-approve-on-protected-main orphaned-finalize bug (plan step b) is real and confirmed in code (ApproveImpl mutation 1/3 closes epic in Dolt; FinalizeEpic `pr` strategy merges via PR before the close reaches main). It is NOT a v0.10.0 blocker (pre-existing, independent of the flatten feature, deterministic recovery exists), so filing the bead is acceptable. BUT it recurs on EVERY future spec finalized on a protected main and is user-facing (downstream users will see closed beads silently reopen on sync) — recommend filing it HIGH priority and adding a 'known limitation' note to the v0.10.0 release notes so downstream protected-main users aren't surprised.
